name: Product Polish — New Commands, UX Moments, Marketing Assets
description: Land everything the product spec promises that isn't code-structure — new commands (`relay logs`, `--help` splash, glossary, `config`), UX moments (ctrl-c pause, --fresh flag, NO_COLOR handling), and marketing-grade artifacts (README hero, per-flow catalog page, landing-page comparison grid, paste-ready copy kit). All outputs conform to `relay-product_spec.md` §4/§5/§6/§8/§12/§14. Every deliverable here consumes the task_77 visual identity module from Sprint 6 so the brand grammar is applied exactly once.

waves.name: relay logs <runId> command
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/logs.ts` — new in product spec §6.1 DIAGNOSTICS, not in tech spec. Reads `<cwd>/.relay/runs/<runId>/run.log` (NDJSON). Pretty-prints each event: `<HH:MM:SS> <level colored> <event> <key=value pairs>`. Respect `--step <id>` to filter, `--follow` / `-f` to tail (fs.watch + append stream), `--level <lvl>` to filter by minimum level. Header: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  logs for <runId>  ·  <flowName>`. Also update task_43's dispatcher registration to wire the command (the `logs` command was added to the dispatcher command list in Sprint 6 wave 1 task_43's updated description). On unknown runId: `✕ no such run` + `→ relay runs` hint. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay splash help
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/help.ts` exporting `renderSplash()`. Output MUST match product spec §6.1 EXACTLY (byte-for-byte): header line `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay · Claude pipelines you can run twice`, blank line, then four category blocks each with the exact copy from §6.1 — USAGE (4 rows), CATALOG (4 rows), AUTHORING (3 rows), DIAGNOSTICS (3 rows), LEARN (2 rows with the mark prefix for links to relay.dev and relay.dev/docs/first-flow). Column alignment so `relay <verb>` on the left and the description on the right read cleanly. Called by `cli.ts` when argv has no args AND no --help flag (product spec says `relay` with no args prints this compact help). Also wire commander so `relay --help` prints this instead of commander's default. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: relay help glossary subcommand
waves.description: Add `relay help glossary` as a dispatcher subcommand per product spec §13. Create `packages/cli/src/commands/glossary.ts` (self-contained — does NOT modify the splash help file from task_79). Output: header `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  glossary`, blank line, then the 5-line glossary from §13 EXACTLY: `flow        a named, versioned pipeline you can run`, `step        one node in a flow (prompt, script, branch, parallel)`, `handoff     the JSON one step produces and a later step consumes`, `run         one execution of a flow; identified by a run id`, `checkpoint  the saved state of a run after each step completes`. Wire as a `help` parent command with a `glossary` subcommand in task_43's dispatcher (the dispatcher already registers nested commands). Independent of task_79's splash; the two help entry points coexist. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: Config command (opt-in plumbing)
waves.description: Create `packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts` — was referenced by task_74 (telemetry) in Sprint 11 but not implemented there. Subcommands: `config get <key>`, `config set <key> <value>`, `config list`. Reads/writes `~/.relay/config.json`. Supported keys: `telemetry.enabled` (boolean), `color` ('auto'|'always'|'never'), `defaultApiKeyOptIn` (boolean; rarely used). Header on list: `●─▶●─▶●─▶●  relay config`. On invalid key: `✕ unknown key '<k>'` plus valid-key list. Wire into task_43's dispatcher (the `config` command was added there). Task_74's telemetry reads from the same file written by this command.

waves.name: Ctrl-C paused UX
waves.description: Install a SIGINT handler in `packages/cli/src/commands/run.ts` (and `resume.ts`) that transitions to the 'paused' display per product spec §11.5. On first ^C: stop the ProgressDisplay, render a PAUSED banner: header with `(paused)` suffix (`●─▶●─▶●─▶●  <flowName> · <runId>  (paused)`), step grid showing each step's final status (with SYMBOLS.cancelled '⊘' for the step that was mid-flight, annotation `cancelled mid-step (turn N)`), summary line `state saved. $<spent> spent.`, blank line, `resume: relay resume <runId>`. This is not an error — exit 0 or 130 (SIGINT convention). Ctrl-C is never rendered as a failure per §11.5 rule. The core Runner's abort logic (task_40) already persists state; this task owns the UI transition. Second ^C within 2s = hard exit. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: --fresh flag on relay run
waves.description: Implement the `--fresh` flag on `relay run` referenced in product spec §6.5 (`run again fresh`) and §6.6 (`relay run <flow> . --fresh`). Behavior: when `--fresh` is passed, generate a new runId regardless of any cached state (no implicit resume), and do NOT auto-install if a prior partial run exists for the same flow+input. Update `packages/cli/src/commands/run.ts` with the flag handler and help text. Document that the default (no --fresh) will resume when a partial run is detected — or rather, `relay run` always makes a new run; `relay resume` is the only resume path. The --fresh flag is therefore a no-op by default but explicit in the CLI so the banner's 'next:' block copy is truthful. Tiny task, add tests that the flag parses and is forwarded to the Runner as a `{ fresh: true }` option.

waves.name: NO_COLOR and --no-color compliance
waves.description: Harden `packages/cli/src/visual.ts` (from task_77) to fully respect the no-color.org convention per product spec §17 #2. Color output MUST be disabled when: (1) `process.env.NO_COLOR` is set to any non-empty value, (2) `--no-color` is passed globally (already declared in task_43's dispatcher), (3) `process.stdout.isTTY` is false, (4) `relay config set color never` has been run (reads `~/.relay/config.json` via task_81). Add an `enableColor(): boolean` helper and export a `colorMode` getter. Every color wrapper in visual.ts passes through raw strings when disabled. Also update Logger (`packages/core/src/logger.ts`) to similarly drop ANSI codes for console output when any of the above are true. Document the precedence in a top-of-file comment (explicit --no-color wins over env, env wins over config, config wins over auto-TTY detection).

waves.name: relay upgrade polish and relay publish banner polish
waves.description: Apply product-spec visual polish to `relay upgrade` and `relay publish` commands (they were implemented plainly in Sprint 7 and Sprint 11 respectively). Each gets the mark header, SYMBOLS.ok/fail rows per action, and a footer `next:` block. Upgrade specifics: show version diff as `<name>  v<old> → v<new>` with colored arrow when new > old (green), gray when same, red when downgrade. Publish specifics: show the `npm publish` command line it will execute BEFORE running (so user can ctrl-c), then run + stream output, then a final SYMBOLS.ok row with the published version and an npm link. Import visuals from task_77.

waves.name: README hero and 60-second tour
waves.description:
Replace the root `README.md` (from task_1) with the product-spec-compliant hero per §7.2 VERBATIM. Centered `

` block with `●─▶●─▶●─▶● relay`, wordmark line with `Claude pipelines you can run twice.`, then the product tagline paragraph: 'Deterministic orchestration. Crash-proof state. Transparent cost. Runs on your Pro/Max subscription — no surprise API bills.' Then `## 60-second tour` section with the three-command bash block from §7.2 (install, doctor, run codebase-discovery). Then the 'Why not X' table from §8.5 with all five rows rendered in GitHub markdown. Then link-out sections to /docs, /flows, /docs/first-flow. License section: MIT (per product spec §18.4). Banned copy: no 'simply', no emojis, no trailing exclamation marks (§4.2).

waves.name: Per-flow catalog page template
waves.description: Create `catalog/flow-template.html` — the per-flow landing page structure from product spec §14.2. Sections in order: mark header + `<h1>` with flow name, one-paragraph description, 4-row metadata grid (`COST`, `DURATION`, `AUDIENCE`, `VERIFIED`/`COMMUNITY` tier badge), horizontal rule, code block with the `$ npx relay run <flow> .` invocation, horizontal rule, three tabs: `[sample output]` / `[how it works]` / `[customize]`. Sample-output tab renders the HTML artifact inline (for codebase-discovery family) or a screenshot (for others). `how it works` enumerates the steps from flow.ts. `customize` gives a one-command fork recipe. Inline CSS, no framework. The catalog site's JS (task_76) renders this template populated from `registry.json` per-flow entries. Include a placeholder `<!-- {{MARK}} -->` etc. for server/JS substitution.

waves.name: Landing page comparison grid
waves.description: Create `catalog/comparison.html` (embedded into `catalog/index.html` via an include or a separate linked section). Renders the §9 comparison grid EXACTLY: four columns (`stateful resume`, `subscription-safe by default`, `pre-built flows`, `Claude-native`) × seven rows (`Relay`, `claude -p shell`, `LangGraph`, `CrewAI`, `SuperClaude`, `claude-pipeline`, `Skills (native)`). Cells use `✓`, `·`, or short text like 'catalog', 'partial', 'depends on env'. Relay row is highlighted. Accompanying H2: 'Where Relay fits' and the §9 closing sentence `Relay is the only tool that fills all four. That's the pitch.` (verbatim). Keep inline CSS minimal, no framework.

waves.name: Paste-ready copy kit
waves.description: Create `docs/copy-kit.md` — the product spec §8 + §18 messaging kit, ready to paste. Sections: (1) Tagline options from §8.1. (2) 140-char pitch from §8.2. (3) HN title from §8.3. (4) Discord pitch from §8.4. (5) 30-second talk pitch from §18.1. (6) GitHub repo description (§18.2). (7) package.json description (§18.3) — ensure this EXACT string is used in `packages/cli/package.json` and `packages/core/package.json`'s description fields (update those files in this task). (8) 'about' paragraph for relay.dev (§18.4). (9) Feature copy blocks from §8.6 (four headlines + one-sentence each). Every string copy-paste-ready, no placeholders. Include a top-of-file contract: `any public copy not in this file is wrong — refer here first.`

waves.name: Naming conventions enforcement and internal glossary
waves.description: Create `docs/naming-conventions.md` documenting the product spec §13 vocabulary as a contract for contributors. The table from §13: flow / step / handoff / run / checkpoint / catalog flow / verified — plus the 'words to avoid' column (pipeline, workflow, task, stage, context, message, session, job, save, state, template, official, recommended). Then an enforcement note: any user-facing copy that introduces a 'words to avoid' term is a bug. Also: scan `packages/*/src/**/*.ts` and `packages/*/README.md` for occurrences of banned terms in USER-FACING strings (CLI output, error messages, README bodies — not code identifiers, not internal comments) and list them as followups in `docs/naming-conventions.md` under a '## Known drift' heading. This is a living document — add a checklist: 'new CLI copy PR → check against this list'.
